sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 30-year-old male, presents with a one-week history of persistent low back pain, which started after a twisting motion on his left side.
He reports that the pain initially went away but returned a few hours later.
The pain is located in the middle to lower back, with occasional radiation to the left side, and is exacerbated by sudden movements to the left.
He denies any fever, chills, or recent trauma.
He has a history of diabetes and takes metformin twice a day.
He has been exercising and trying to control his sugar levels.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes.
Review of systems: The patient reports occasional trouble with his left eye, but it is not a primary concern at this visit.
Current medications: Metformin 2 times a day.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's lungs are clear, and his heart rate and rhythm are regular.
The physical exam revealed tenderness in the middle to lower back, particularly in the left side, with some radiation to the left gluteal area.
The patient's lower extremities are without pain, numbness, or tingling.
Test Results: Not mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I believe the patient has a musculoskeletal low back strain.
Problem list: Low back strain; Diabetes; [NOT MENTIONED].
Plan:
I ordered a low back x-ray to evaluate the joint spaces and potential disk issues.
I also ordered physical therapy to help with pain management and strengthen the back muscles.
I prescribed 400mg of ibuprofen as needed for pain, up to 3 times a day, and 1 tablet of flexeril 1 time a day in the evening for muscle relaxation.
I want the patient to continue with his current diabetes management, including diet, exercise, and metformin.
I will order an A1c to evaluate his diabetes control.
I will refer him to a pain doctor if the pain persists after the x-ray and physical therapy.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the x-ray and A1c, and to reassess his diabetes management.
